title: Nasdaq snaps 13-day streak as Dow, S&P 500 decline modestly
body:
The US stock market experienced a modest downturn on Monday, as the Nasdaq's impressive 13-day winning streak came to an end. The Dow and S&P 500 also closed lower, amid growing concerns over the ongoing conflict in Iran, which has now entered its eighth week. As the latest cease-fire deadline looms, investors are becoming increasingly cautious, leading to a sell-off in the market. The decline was not drastic, but it marked a significant shift in sentiment, as investors weighed the potential implications of the escalating tensions in the Middle East.

The war in Iran has been a major concern for investors, as it has the potential to disrupt global oil supplies and spark a wider conflict in the region. The latest cease-fire deadline has added to the uncertainty, as investors wait to see if the warring parties will be able to reach a agreement. The lack of progress in the negotiations has led to a decrease in investor confidence, causing many to reevaluate their positions in the market. The Nasdaq, which had been on a tear in recent days, was particularly hard hit, as technology stocks are often sensitive to changes in investor sentiment.

The decline in the market was not uniform, however, as some sectors were able to withstand the selling pressure. Defensives, such as consumer staples and healthcare, were among the better performers, as investors sought out safer havens. The energy sector was also a bright spot, as oil prices rose on concerns over supply disruptions. However, these gains were not enough to offset the losses in other sectors, such as technology and finance, which were hit hard by the sell-off. Overall, the market's decline was a reflection of the growing uncertainty and caution among investors.

The ongoing conflict in Iran has significant implications for the global economy, and investors are right to be concerned. The country is a major oil producer, and any disruption to its supplies could have far-reaching consequences. The US, in particular, is vulnerable to changes in the global oil market, as it is a major importer of crude oil. The conflict also has the potential to spark a wider regional conflict, which could have devastating consequences for the global economy. As such, investors are likely to remain cautious in the coming days, as they wait to see how the situation develops.

As the latest cease-fire deadline approaches, investors will be watching the developments in Iran closely. Any sign of progress in the negotiations could lead to a rebound in the market, as investor confidence returns. However, if the conflict were to escalate, it could lead to a more significant sell-off, as investors become increasingly risk-averse. The US Federal Reserve will also be closely watching the situation, as it considers its next move on interest rates. The central bank has been cautious in its approach to monetary policy, and the ongoing conflict in Iran is likely to reinforce this caution.

Looking ahead, the situation in Iran is likely to remain a major concern for investors in the coming days. The latest cease-fire deadline is a critical juncture in the conflict, and the outcome will have significant implications for the global economy. As such, investors will need to remain vigilant, as they navigate the uncertain landscape. While the decline in the market on Monday was modest, it serves as a reminder of the risks and uncertainties that are present in the global economy. As the situation continues to unfold, investors will need to be prepared for a range of possible outcomes, and to adjust their strategies accordingly.
